item.title: Eating for Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Assessing the Effect of Dietary Carbohydrates on Gut Microbiota and Implications for Disease Management.
item.description: The Journal of nutrition - Targeting the gut microbiome through diet remains a priority in the treatment and management of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) for patients and clinicians alike. However, expert consensus is lac...

item.title: Microbial metabolite Enterobactin impairs mitochondrial respiration and alleviates colitis.
item.description: Gut microbes - Host-microbe interactions critically shape cellular metabolism and immune responses. The bacterial siderophore enterobactin (Ent) is known for pilfering iron from the host, but recent evidence sugg...

item.title: Parabacteroides distasonis-derived extracellular vesicles alleviate ulcerative colitis by regulating tryptophan metabolism to activates AhR.
item.description: Microbiological research - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease characterized by impaired intestinal barrier function. Previous studies have indicated that Parabacteroides distasonis (P. distasonis) exer...

item.title: Caspase 5c amplifies Wnt via APC cleavage to promote intestinal homeostasis.
item.description: Nature - Caspase 5 (CASP5) is a member of the inflammatory caspase family of cysteine proteases that is involved in inflammation and cell death1-3. CASP5 shares the highest homology with inflammatory CASP4,...

item.title: Interleukin-23 Inhibitors in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: BioDrugs : clinical immunotherapeutics, biopharmaceuticals and gene therapy - Review - Selective inhibition of interleukin-23 (IL-23) has emerged as a highly effective therapeutic strategy in inflammatory bowel disease, targeting a central pathway of chronic intestinal inflammation w...

item.title: Effects of different galactans on DSS-induced colitis and the gut microbiota.
item.description: Food & function - Bioactive galactans are widely distributed in nature, with some extensively reported for their potential in modulating the gut microbiota and intestinal inflammation. However, the efficacy of diffe...

item.title: Characterization of the Gut Virome in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease.
item.description: Journal of inflammation research - The dysbiosis of the gut microbiota is a well-known correlate in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the microbiome characteristics of patients with IBD who also have non...

item.title: Wumei Wan alleviates intestinal fibrosis via the microbiota-metabolite-YAP-mechanotransduction axis.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Intestinal fibrosis is a common complication of Crohn's disease, for which effective therapies remain lacking. Wumei Wan (WMW), a classical traditional Chinese medicine formula, has been used for n...

item.title: Chlorella pyrenoidosa-derived extracellular vesicles ameliorate ulcerative colitis through microbiota-mediated AKT/mTOR/ferroptosis pathway.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease characterized by frequent relapses and slow recovery, with a current deficiency in safe and effective therapeutic options. Chlorella pyreno...

item.title: Prodrug-integrated multifunctional liposome encapsulation enhances probiotic-based bacteriotherapy for inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Materials today. Bio - Probiotic-based bacteriotherapies show promise for treating inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) by dynamically modulating the intestinal microenvironment. However, their clinical efficacy is hampered ...

item.title: Th17/treg balance in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: the role of microbial, and genetic regulators in disease modulation.
item.description: Frontiers in cell and developmental biology - Review - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is a chronic condition characterized by persistent mucosal inflammation driven by complex interactions among the gut microbiome, host immune genetics, and cellular ...

item.title: Mode of birth and risk of inflammatory bowel disease in offspring: an updated systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Frontiers in reproductive health - Review - Cesarean section (CS) rates continue rising worldwide, raising concerns about long-term offspring health consequences, including inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). This systematic review and meta-an...

item.title: Gut microbiota and bile acids profiles study of ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease patients.
item.description: Frontiers in microbiology - The global incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is increasing, with dysbiosis of the gut microbiota identified as a significant factor contributing to chronic intestinal inflammation. This...

item.title: From mucus secretion to immune surveillance: the evolving roles of goblet cells in intestinal homeostasis and inflammation.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - Intestinal goblet cells (GCs) are specialized epithelial cells essential for forming the protective mucus barrier. Recent research has significantly expanded our understanding of their roles beyond...

item.title: A novel Curcuma wenyujin-derived fructan modulates gut microbiota and metabolic pathways to ameliorate DSS-induced colitis.
item.description: Carbohydrate polymers - Ulcerative colitis (UC) involves epithelial barrier breakdown, dysregulated mucosal immunity, and dysbiosis of the gut microbiota (GM). Given the biotherapeutic potential of dietary fructans, this ...
